one_line_definition: A 2×2 matrix relating displacement and stress states on either side of a thin interface, encapsulating its rheological and inertial properties
Boundary conditions that allow small jumps in stress or displacement across an interface, replacing classical continuity conditions
Complex stiffness of an interface per unit area (units: GPa/mm or Pa/m), defined as stress jump divided by relative displacement jump
Condition under which a layer of thickness d is acoustically equivalent to an interface: the product of wave number and thickness is much less than one
The moment during polymer cure when the loss tangent (G''/G') equals one, marking transition from viscous liquid to elastic network
A rheological model with a spring in parallel with a Maxwell element, producing frequency-dependent complex modulus: K* = K0 + K1[jωτ/(1+jωτ)]
Ratio of reflected to incident acoustic wave amplitude at an interface or layered structure; function of acoustic impedance contrasts and geometry
Product of material density and acoustic wave velocity; determines how much a wave reflects vs. transmits at an interface
A dip in the Fourier-domain reflection spectrum caused by destructive interference of multiple internal echoes within a bondline at frequencies satisfying d = nλ/2
A description of acoustic energy loss (dB/cm) as a function of frequency in the bondline material; affects both pulse shape and spectral depression depth
A model of adhesively bonded structures as: adherend / interfacial layer / adhesive / interfacial layer / adherend, replacing the simpler 3-layer version to capture adhesion failure
Recursive calculation of the effective acoustic impedance seen by an incident wave at each layer interface of a multilayer stack, used to compute the overall reflection coefficient
The frequency width of the spectral depression around a resonance minimum; found to be proportional to bond quality — wider for stronger bonds
Discretization of the acoustic wave equation in space (finite elements) and time (explicit central-difference stepping) to compute mechanical displacement fields in bonded structures
Measurement configuration where the same transducer emits and receives ultrasonic pulses; time-of-flight and amplitude of echoes encode material properties
The characteristic S-shaped rise of longitudinal wave velocity during polymer curing, reflecting the liquid-to-crosslinked-solid transformation
High-frequency ultrasound imaging technique producing spatial maps (C-scans) of acoustic amplitude or time-of-flight to visualize microstructure or defects
NDE technique that measures the electrical impedance of a bonded PZT transducer; structural and bonding-layer resonances modulate the impedance spectrum
Non-contact electromagnetic inspection technique (0.1–3 THz) used in reflection C-scan mode to image the spatial distribution of adhesive layers beneath surfaces
Partial or full loss of adhesive contact between a piezoelectric sensor and its host structure, detectable by emergence of free-transducer resonance peaks in EMI spectra
Continuous or periodic automated monitoring of structural integrity using permanently installed sensor networks
